core_mechanism: large-deviations error exponents for binary hypothesis testing — the exponential decay rate of the discrimination error probability between two candidate distributions, characterized by Chernoff/Hoeffding-type divergence bounds, extended from independent to weakly-correlated sequences via a factorization (subadditivity) property
brief_reason: The abstract centers on quantum Chernoff and Hoeffding bounds for the error exponents of a binary hypothesis-testing problem, generalizing them from product (independent) states to correlated states that obey a factorization property.
